viral_posts.hook: peace comes from focusing on what you can control.
viral_posts.caption:
peace comes from focusing on what you can control.

you can’t control what happens around you. but you can control your health. your mood. your experiences. how you show up for yourself every day.

the moment you stop trying to control everything and start focusing only on what’s in your hands, everything shifts.
less anxiety. more clarity. actual peace.

viral_posts.hook: AI is here. Start building your personal brand before is too late.
viral_posts.caption: AI is here. Start building your personal brand before is too late.

viral_posts.hook: Nobody ever got rich from being an employee. here’s why:
viral_posts.caption: Nobody ever got rich from being an employee. here’s why:

viral_posts.hook: I was standing in the Dolomites last October when it hit me.
viral_posts.caption:
I was standing in the Dolomites last October when it hit me.

The air was cold. The mountains were massive. And for the first time in a long time, I felt completely still.

No emails. No Slack. No fires to put out.

Just silence.

And in that silence, I remembered why I started all of this in the first place.

Not to work less. I love building. I love the game.

But years ago, I almost lost sight of what winning actually looks like.

I was making money. Growing companies. Hitting targets.

But I was also exhausted. Drained. Running on fumes.

The business was thriving, but my soul wasn’t.

That’s when I realized the problem wasn’t the work itself.
It was how I was working.

Stuck in operations. Drowning in decisions that didn’t need me. Burning energy on tasks that someone else could handle.

Not building anymore. Just managing.

So I rebuilt everything.

Not to work less. But to work on what actually matters.

Now I have energy for the business I love and the life I want.

These mountains. The work that lights me up. The freedom to choose.

That’s what I’m building toward.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

viral_posts.hook: This was me before anyone told me to tone it down.
viral_posts.caption:
This was me before anyone told me to tone it down.

Before ambition had to be practical.
Before big ideas needed approval.
Before safe started sounding smarter than bold.

At some point we call it “maturing.”

But what really happens is the edges get softened.

The instinct to build something unreasonable.
The desire to live on your own terms.
The willingness to look foolish in pursuit of something bigger.

That part doesn’t disappear.

It just gets buried under expectations.

And when you feel that pull watching someone build freely, create openly, live differently…

That’s not envy.

That’s a reminder.

The original version of you is still there.

viral_posts.hook: Remember why you got in the game.
viral_posts.caption:

For most founders it comes down to one word:

Freedom.

viral_posts.caption:
Comment “FOUNDER” for the direct link to Episode 26 🎙️

Matt has helped thousands of founders build profitable audiences online.
We go deep on content systems, lead magnets, and how to turn attention into revenue.

viral_posts.hook: Before you win, people question you.
viral_posts.caption:
Before you win, people question you.

Why are you working so hard?
Why sacrifice nights out for this?

Your discipline looks unnecessary.
Your ambition looks extreme.

After you win, everything changes.
Suddenly it was all luck.

“Right place, right time.”
“Things just worked out for you.”

What they won’t acknowledge is the grind.

The years you showed up when you didn’t feel like it.
The risks you took when no one believed in you.
The patience you held when results were nowhere in sight.

Before success, they call you obsessed.
After success, they call you lucky.

Let them talk either way.
Keep building.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

viral_posts.hook: Self esteem is the reputation you have with yourself.
viral_posts.caption:
Self esteem is the reputation you have with yourself.

You’re watching yourself at all times. 

You know what you’re doing.

If you don’t live up to your own moral code, the same one you hold others to, it damages your self esteem.

So how do you build it back up?

Live up to your own standards. Rigorously.

Do things for others without expecting anything back.

The moments you actually will be proud of aren’t what you’d expect.

Not the material success. 
Not the achievements.

It’s when you make sacrifices for people you love.

Those are the moments that build real confidence.

viral_posts.hook: i’m 36… it took me way too long to realize this:
viral_posts.caption: i’m 36… it took me way too long to realize this:

Stop posting random content.

Here’s what actually works:

Top-of-Funnel: Attracts new people. Built for reach and discovery.

Bottom-of-Funnel: Creates decisions. Points people toward action.

You need both.

TOF brings them in. 
BOF moves them forward.

If all your content is TOF, you’re just entertaining.

If all your content is BOF, you’re not reaching anyone new.

Stop posting randomly.

Start posting strategically.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
